sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old female, presents with complaints of ankle pain, which began after slipping and twisting her left ankle two days ago.
She reports falling onto the outside of her ankle, resulting in swelling and pain, making it difficult to walk.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with minimal relief.
She denies bleeding and reports pain primarily on the outside of her ankle, with no pain radiating to her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is currently taking metformin for diabetes and norvasc for high blood pressure.
Her hemoglobin A1c was 6.8 last recorded, and she is due for a follow-up eye exam for her diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient reports no additional symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: Metformin, norvasc.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70, oxygen saturation [NOT MENTIONED], temperature [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam is fine, with no bruits.
Her lungs are clear, and her heart exam is normal, except for a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, which is unchanged from previous exams.
Her belly exam is good.
On her ankle exam, she has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling and redness.
She has normal sensation and pulses in her lower extremities.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has an ankle sprain, but an x-ray is needed to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; High blood pressure; Diabetes mellitus.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the patient's ankle to rule out a fracture.
I will place her in an air splint and have her use crutches until the results are back.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I instructed her to keep her ankle elevated, apply ice, and avoid weight-bearing.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
I also refilled her norvasc and ordered baseline labs, including a hemoglobin A1c, to check on her diabetes management.
I advised her to continue her metformin and attend her scheduled eye exam.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the x-ray results are back, and if necessary, to reassess her ankle and adjust her treatment plan.
